findings.evidence: Grep shows only the raw definitions and constructors: lines 142-143 define `AmbientIsotopic`, line 150 has `of_ambientIsotopy`, and lines 185-188 define `ambientIsotopicSetoid`; no `_def`/`_iff` lemma is present.
findings.fix: Add API such as `ambientIsotopic_def : AmbientIsotopic f g ↔ ∃ Φ, Φ.final.comp f = g` and `@[simp] ambientIsotopicSetoid_r_iff : (ambientIsotopicSetoid X Y).r f g ↔ AmbientIsotopic f g`.
findings.issue: `AmbientIsotopic` and `ambientIsotopicSetoid` are exposed without characteristic iff/simp restatements, so users must unfold the definitions to get the ambient-isotopy witness or relate the setoid relation back to `AmbientIsotopic`.

findings.evidence: `final_trans` is `@[simp]` at lines 92-93, while `final_symm_final` at line 119 and `final_final_symm` at line 127 have no automation attribute.
findings.issue: The inverse-final cancellation lemmas are normal forms but are not marked `@[simp]`, unlike `final_trans`, so automation will not simplify `Φ.symm.final (Φ.final y)` or `Φ.final (Φ.symm.final y)` to `y`.

summary: The exposed relation and operations are close, but the public API still makes downstream users rely on unfolding for the relation/setoid and lacks simp normal forms for the inverse final map.
